name: Dependency Mapper
description: Reads the stack audit contract and maps all dependency relationships: direct and peer dependencies, CVE-based security advisories per package, deprecated or unmaintained packages, packages with 0 maintainers, circular dependencies, and the hardest-to-migrate dependencies. Escalates if any CRITICAL deprecated dependency (e.g. end-of-life framework) is found.

checks.pass: Stack audit contract available — ready to map dependencies.
checks.fail: Stack audit contract not found. Cannot map dependencies without stack audit.

checks.prompt: Using the stack audit provided, map all dependency relationships in detail. Produce a dependency map covering: (1) direct and peer dependency graph — which packages depend on which, (2) security advisories per package using CVE data from the audit — list CVE IDs, severity, and affected versions, (3) deprecated packages — packages that are no longer actively maintained, with last publish date and deprecation notice if available, (4) packages with 0 maintainers — flag any package where the maintainer count has dropped to zero (supply chain risk), (5) circular dependencies — identify any circular dependency chains that would complicate migration, (6) hardest-to-migrate dependencies — rank the top 5 dependencies that would be most costly or risky to replace, with justification. CRITICAL: if any dependency is an end-of-life framework or runtime with no active security patching, flag as CRITICAL_DEPRECATED and set noCriticalDeprecations to false.
checks.pass: Dependency map complete — no critical deprecated dependencies found.
checks.fail: CRITICAL deprecated dependency found. Migration risk is HIGH. Escalating before assessment proceeds.
